core_payload.content:
# Agent Instruction Surfaces

## 1. Role
Every serious N1Hub agent enters the repository through a durable local instruction stack before any large tool loop begins. Repository law, doctrine summaries, indexes, and machine-readable exports remain always-present operating surfaces instead of optional prompt cargo.

## 2. Instruction Stack
- `AGENTS.md`, `SOUL.md`, `TOOLS.md`, and `WORKFLOW.md` acting as an explicit layered instruction surface
- machine-readable exports such as full-vault and codebase index packs
- concise always-on surfaces that still preserve broad project literacy
- lane-declared load order so every major swarm lane states which instruction surfaces govern it
- control-plane awareness of which instruction stack applies to each assignment family

## 3. Operational Consequence
Agents enter N1Hub already oriented to repository law, project structure, capsule doctrine, and blast-radius constraints. Skills remain procedural, while instruction surfaces carry broad repository and framework truth.

## 4. Philosophical Alignment
Agent Instruction Surfaces supports To Dig Deep by making versioned local doctrine the default onboarding path for every serious agent lane.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Agent Instruction Surfaces defines the durable local instruction stack that every serious N1Hub agent should load before entering a large tool loop. It covers repository law, doctrine summaries, compact indexes, machine-readable exports, lane-declared load order, and explicit governance over which surfaces guide each assignment family. The capsule matters because agents arrive oriented to project structure, capsule doctrine, and blast-radius limits before they improvise. Skills remain procedural overlays, while instruction surfaces carry broader repository truth that stays versioned, local, and reviewable.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Removed meta-language so the capsule states current doctrine directly.
